lesson.menu_desc: Present perfect for results and persuasion to demonstrate value and renew the contract -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 19 -- Renewals & Driving Value
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Patrícia Xavier Lima Iwashita | Aula 19 -- Renewals & Driving Value

listenings.text: Hi Patrícia, this is David, leaving a quick note before our renewal call. Our contract is up next month, and honestly, my budget is tight this year. Finance has asked me a hard question: what value have we really got from this partnership? So before we renew, I need to see it clearly. Show me the numbers. How much has adoption grown? How many hours has my team saved? What is the return on investment? If you can demonstrate a strong track record, I am confident we can justify the renewal to finance. And if the value is really there, I am open to hearing about an upsell too. Let us go through the results together on the call. Talk soon.

Hi Patrícia, it is Sofia. Before your renewal call, here is what I have learned about driving value. A renewal is not a favour you ask for; it is a result you have earned. So always lead with results, not with a price. Say what you have delivered: we have grown adoption, we have reduced churn, your team has saved real time. Use the present perfect, because these results are still true today. Then connect each result to a reason the customer cares about, and let the numbers justify the renewal for you. Once you have demonstrated the value, it is the right moment to suggest an upsell. Show the track record first, and the partnership almost renews itself. You will do great. Talk soon.
